allow: Bash(bash .specify/scripts/bash/create-new-feature.sh "Build an AI-Orchestrator that interfaces with the Obsidian Local REST API. The application must be able to: Read & Analyze: Parse Markdown and Frontmatter from specific files or folders. Surgical Edit: Use JSON Patch \(RFC 6902\) to append backlinks, update tags, or modify metadata without touching the rest of the note body. Command Execution: Trigger native Obsidian commands \(like Save or Template Insertion\) via API. State Management: Track long-running vault scans using a task queue. The UI should be a simple FastAPI-generated Swagger/Redoc interface for monitoring agent logs and triggering manual Vault Cleanup jobs." --json --short-name "ai-orchestrator")

Bash(.specify/scripts/bash/create-new-feature.sh "Build a vault health analytics system that tracks orphan notes, tag distribution, backlink density, and cluster connectivity over time. Surface a dashboard endpoint with current metrics and historical trends. Integrate with the existing job system for scheduled scans." --json --short-name "vault-health-analytics" 2>&1)

Bash(python3 -m ruff check app/models/health_snapshot.py app/schemas/health.py app/services/health_service.py app/tasks/vault_health_scan.py app/api/routes/vault_health.py app/api/routes/jobs.py app/celery_app.py app/main.py app/config.py app/models/job.py 2>&1 || true)

Bash(.specify/scripts/bash/create-new-feature.sh "Build an auto-triage system that periodically scans recent notes and suggests missing tags, backlinks, and required frontmatter fields based on folder conventions. Low-risk fixes auto-apply through the existing patch system; suggestions for high-risk changes queue for approval." --json --short-name "auto-triage")
